title: [Arc] Add basic LLVM lowering
body:
Add two passes to lower a design to a software model.

The `LowerClocksToFuncs` pass outlines all `arc.clock_tree` and `arc.passthrough` operations into separate MLIR functions. This conceptually converts clocks from being a signal in the design into a function that can be called in order to execute the state update triggered by that clock.

The `LowerArcToLLVM` conversion pass does exactly as it says: it sets up a dialect conversion from Arc and the core CIRCT dialects to Func/SCF, and from there to the LLVM dialect.

Also add an output format option to the arcilator tool that allows for the direct emission of LLVM IR (as opposed to the MLIR's LLVM dialect).
